transliteration: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na a-la-ḫi-im e-la-ma pu-šu-ke-en6 ù a-šur-ta-ak-la-ku qí-bi-ma a-ḫu-a a-tù-nu iš-tù MU.ŠÈ 30 i-na a-lim{ki} wa-áš-ba-ku-ma ù ni-kà-sí ù-ša-qal kà-ra-am mu-ùḫ-ra-ma iš-tí kà-ri-im e-na-na-tim er-ša-nim-ma a-na KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na-um 3 GÍN la-dí a šu-ma la i-ma-gu5-ru sà-li-a-ma a-mì-šál a-we-el la-zi-iz a-tù-nu la tí-de8-a-a a-i-a-tum ma-áš-kà-na-tù-a e-la ma-la ma-áš-kà-tám la áš-ku-nam 37 ma-na ni-kà-sí áš-qúl gi-mì-lam i-ṣé-ri-a šu-ku-na iḫ-da-ma a-na mì-šál a-we-el la-zi-iz a-ni-tum la a-ni-tum té-er-ta-ak-nu i pá-ni-e-ma li-li-kam iš-tí KÙ.BABBAR-pí-a 4.5 ma-na ša ku-ru-ub-IŠTAR ba-áb-tám e-zi-ba-ku-nu-tí-ni ša-áš-qí-la-ma šé-bi-la-nim
translation: From Aššur-idi to Alâhum, Elamma, Pnsu-kén and Aššur-taldâku: My dear brothers, for 30 years I have been living in the City and I always pay the accounts. Appeal to the colony authorities and ask for clemency for me from the colony, so that for the silver I may deposit 3 shekels per mina. If they do not agree with you, then implore them that I may stand only for half a share of a man. Are you not aware which are my deposits? Should I make another deposit on top of that? I have paid 37 minas on the account! Do me this favour. Take care to have me stand only for half a share of a man Send me word whether this is so or not as soon as possible. Together with my other silvet I left 4 1/2 minas belonging to Kurub-Istar as an outstanding claim with you. Have it paid and send it to me.

transliteration: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na a-mur-IŠTAR a-la-ḫi-im ì-lí-a-lim ù a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku qí-bi4-ma 2 GÚ 1 ma-na AN.NA-ki ku-nu-ku 38 TÚG.ḪI.A ú-ṣú-ur-ša-a-šùr ub-lá-ku-nu-tí ig-ri sà-ri-dim ša-bu mì-ma lá ta-da-na-šu-um i-na pá-ni-im ṭup-pí-a áš-pu-ra-ku-nu-tí um-ma a-na-ku-ma AN.NA ù TÚG.ḪI.A a-na ì-lí-a-lim dí-na mì-ma la ta-da-na-šu-um i-na ša šé-pì-šu AN.NA ma-dam il5-té-qé i-na TÚG.ḪI-tí-a ma-aṭ-ú-tim li-iq-a-ma a-na ni-kà-sí-a qá-tí i-ta-dí-a ṣí-tí TÚG.ḪI-tí-a ù 2 GÚ AN.NA a-na a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku dí-na-ma a-šar KÙ.BABBAR 1 GÍN ba-lá-ṭí-a lu-bi4-il5 šu-ma lá-šu a-šar wa-áš-bu šé-bi4-lá-šu-um šu-ma ḫa-ra-šu a-ni AN.NA-ki ù TÚG.ḪI-tí-a i-na ṣé-er DAM.GÀR ke-nim a-na u4-me id-a DAM.GÀR lu ke-en6 u4-me 1 ITU.KAM ù 2 ITU.KAM lá ta-be-e-lá a-na ì-lí-a-lim qí-bi4-ma ší-tí KÙ.BABBAR pá-ni-im 2 ma-na i-na pá-ni-im-ma šé-bi4-lam lu-qú-tám ša šé-pí-kà za-ki-a-ma tí-ib-a-ma a-ta-al-kam ṭup-pu-um a-ni-um wa-ar-ki
translation: From Aššur-idi to Amur-Istar, Ala.hum, Ili-alum and Aššur-taklaku: talents 10 minas of tin under seal (and) 38 textiles Usur-sa-Aššur has brought to you. He has been paid the wages for a donkeydriver; do not give him anything. In my former letter I wrote you as follows: "Give the tin and textiles to Ili-alum." But do not give him anything! He took a lot of tin from his own carav an. Out of the textiles take those of lesser quality and deposit them as my share on my account. Give the rest of my textiles and the 2 talents of tin to Aaaur-taldäku, let him bring that to where there is even a small profit for me. If he is absent, then send it to him where he is staying. If he is on a journey, well, then entrust my tin and my textiles with a trustworthy agent in commission . on fixed terms. The agent must be reliable! Do not attach importance to terms of one or two months. To Ili-alum: Send me the rest of the previous silver shipment, minas as soon as possible. Clear the goods from your own caravan, set out and come here. This tablet is later...

transliteration: um-ma a-šur-i-dí-ma a-na a-lá-ḫi-im a-šur-na-da ì-lí-a-lim ù a-šur-ta-ak-lá-ku qí-bi-ma 8 {TÚG}ku-ta-nu i-a-ú-tum 1.5 ma-na AN.NA a qá-tí-šu a-dí-in ḫa-mu-uš-tí ANŠE mì-ma a-nim i-a-um 1 {TÚG}ku-ta-nu-um ša ṭá-áb-ṣí-li-a-šur 1 ku-tí-nim ša IŠTAR-pí-lá-aḫ a-na ì-lí-a-lim dí-na mì-ma a-nim a-lá-ḫu-um na-áš-a-ku-nu-tí 8 TÚG a-ni-ú-tim ù 15 TÚG.ḪI.A ša en-nu-um-be-lúm ub-lá-ku-nu-tí-ni ŠU.NÍGIN 23 TÚG.ḪI.A a-ni-ú-tim a-na ni-kà-sí-a a-na É kà-ri-im i-ta-dí-a a-na ì-lí-a-lim qí-bi-ma lu-qú-ut-kà lu pá-ni-tám lu wa-ar-ki-tám za-ki-am-ma tí-ib-am-ma a-tal-kam a-pu-tum i-nu-mì KÙ.BABBAR ša a-lim{ki} iš-ta-pu-ku-ni qá-tám i-a-tám ù ša a-šur-na-da šu-uq-lá a-wa-at a-lim{ki} da-na-at a-pu-tum iḫ-da-ma KÙ.BABBAR šu-uq-lá
translation: From Aššur-idi to Alâhum, Assur-nâdâ, Ili-âlum and Aššur-taklâku: The 8 textiles are mine; I gave him 1 mina of tin for expenses; one-fifth of a donkey—all this belongs to me. 1 kutânu-textile belongs to Tab-sill-Aššur; 1 kutinu-textile belongs to Istar-pilah—give that to Ili-alum. Alâhum is on his way to you with all this. These 8 textiles plus 15 textiles which Ennam-Bélum has brought to youin all: these 23 textiles— you must deposit on my account in the colony office. To Ili-âlum: Clear your merchandise, both the new and the earlier shipment, set out and come here. Please, when they make deposits of silver belonging to the City, pay both my share and that of Aššur-nâdâ. u.e. The order of the City is strict! Please, take care to pay the silver!

transliteration: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na ì-lí-SUKKAL ù a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku qí-bi4-ma a-na ì-lí-ŠU-GAN qí-bi4-ma ma-áš-ki ša-pá-tim a-dì-na-kum um-ma a-ta-ma a-na ru-ba-im a-qí-áš ù 2 ma-na ḫu-ša-e uṣ-ba-ku-um um-ma a-ta-ma pá-šu-ra-am SIG5 ša 10 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR a-dá-na-ku-um šu-ma a-ḫi a-ta pá-šu-ra-am SIG5 a-na a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku dí-in a-na a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku qí-bi4-ma 3 ma-na 12 GÍN AN.NA a-na ILLAT šu-ku-un ší-im URUDU ša a-na a-mu-re-e áš-qú-lu i-na ṭup-pì-im iš-tí tap-pá-e-a lá-pì-it ì-lí-šu-GAN DUMU na-ni ṣa-ba-at-ma pá-šu-ra-am pá-ni-a-ma ša-dí-in
translation: From Aššur-idi to Ili-sukkal and Aššur-taklâku: (specifically) to Ill-sukkal: I gave you some woollen fleeces. You said: "I shall give them as a gift to the king." I furthermore added for you 2 minas of scrap metal. You said: "I shall give you good quality retail goods worth 10 shekels of silver in return." My dear brother, give the good quality retail goods to Aššur-taklâku. To Aššur-taklâku: Deliver 3 minas 12 shekels of tin to the enterprise. The price of the copper, which I paid to the Amorites, has been booked in the tablet together with my partners. Seize Ili-sukkal son of Nanni and make him hand over the good quality retail goods at once.

transliteration: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na PUZUR4.IŠTAR ù ás-qú-dim qí-bi4-ma 1 GÚ 5 ma-na AN.NA ku-nu-ki ša ás-qú-dum na-áš-ú a-na-ma tí-me-el-ki-a ta-kà-ša-dá-ni um-ma a-tù-nu-ma a-na šu-IŠTAR DUMU a-zu-da qí-bi4-a-ma té-er-tí a-šùr-i-dí AN.NA 1 GÚ li-qé-ma gi-mì-lam i-na ṣé-ri-šu šu-ku-un ṭup-pu-šu ḫa-ar-ma-am ša ḫu-bu-li-šu lu-up-ta a-na 25 ḫa-am-ša-tim šu-ma lá i-ma-ga-ar-ku-nu a-na 30 lu 35 ḫa-am-ša-tim lu-up-ta ṭup-pu-šu ḫi-ir-ma-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da pì-iq-da ù TÚG.ḪI.A ma-lá ta-le-e-a-ni ad-ma-ta-ni ší-tí TÚG.ḪI.A a-na ṣé-er a-šùr-na-da bi4-il5 šu-ma a-ḫu-a-a a-tù-nu ḫi-da-ma gi-mì-lam i-ṣé-ri-a šu-uk-na a-na ás-qú-dim qí-bi4-ma šu-ma PUZUR4.IŠTAR a-ta-ma té-bi-ir ší-bi4 3 šu-ku-šu-um
translation: From Aššur-idi to Puzur-Ištar cpd Asgndum: As to the 1 talent 5 minas of tin under seal which Asqùdum is bringing, as soon as you reach Timelkia you must say as follows to Sû-Ištar, the son of Azuda: "Aššur-idi's orders were that you should take at least 1 talent of tin to do him a favour." Draw up a certified tablet concerning his debt to run for 25 weeks. If he does not agree with you, then draw it up for 30 or 35 weeks. Certify his tablet and entrust it to Aššur-nâdâ. Also, make him take as many textiles as cp investment as you ccp. Bring the rest of the textiles to Aššur-nâdâ. If you are truly my brothers, take care cpd do me this favour. To Asgndum: If Puzur-Ištar <is absent>, then hand it over personally to him Set 3 witnesses for him.

transliteration: um-ma a-šùr-i-dì-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi-ma 10 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR tù-šé-bi-lam ŠÀ.BA 2 GÚ 10 ma-na ku-nu-ku 16.5 GÍN.TA KÙ.BABBAR-pí-šu 7.83333 ma-na 2.6666600000000003 GÍN 4 TÚG šu-ru-tum 5 {TÚG}ku-ta-nu 0.5 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR it-bu-lu 7 GÍN i-na ma-sà-i-im im-tì 17 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ší-im 1 ANŠE 5 GÍN ú-nu-sú 0.33333 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ší-im 2 TÚG šu-ru-en ša a-šùr-ta-ak-lá-ku e-BA.ZI-ku-ni um-ma a-ta-ma i-na 1.3333300000000001 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ša en-na-be-lúm na-áš-ú-ni le-qé KÙ.BABBAR ú-la al-qé i-na KÙ.BABBAR a-nim al-qé 12 ma-na 5 GÍN AN.NA-ak qá-tim 15 GÍN.TA 0.66666 ma-na 8.33333 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR-pí-šu mì-ma a-nim a-šùr-ta-ak-la-ku i-ra-dì-a-kum gám-ra-kum
translation: Aššwt-idi to Aššwt-nâdâ: You have sent me 10 minas of silver. Specification: 2 talents 10 minas (of tin) under seal at the rate 16 1/2 to 1 - in silver: 7 5/6 minas 2 2/3 shekels. black textiles (cpd) 5 kutânu-textiles cost 1/2 mina of silver. It diminished 7 shekels during the washing. 17 shekels of silver: the price of one donkey. shekels: its harness. 1/3 mina of silver: the price of black textiles that Aššwt-taklâku left with you. You said (earlier): "Take that out of the 1 1/3 mina of silver that Ennam-Bélum is bringing." I did not take the silver then,, (so) I have taken it out of this silver. minas 5 shekels of tin for expenses at the rate 15 to 1 - in silver: 2/3 mina 8 1/3 shekels of silver. Aššwt-taklâku brings all this to you.

transliteration: um-ma a-šur-i-dí-ma a-na a-šur-na-da qí-bi4-ma 2 ma-na LÁ 0.5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR IŠTAR-pì-lá-aḫ tù-šé-bi4-lam 1 ma-na 0.5 GÍN i-na ša šu-IŠTAR al-qé ŠU.NÍGIN 3 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ŠÀ.BA 7.5 GÍN ni-is-ḫa-tim áš-qúl iš-tù ša-du-ú-tum ga-am-ra-at-ni 1 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR a-pu-ul ší-tí KÙ.BABBAR-pì-kà 2.83333 ma-na 1.5 GÍN 8 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR iš-tí DUMU a-bu-SILIM ù tap-pá-i-šu al-qé 2.6666600000000003 ma-na 1.25 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR ší-im TÚG.ḪI.A ša šé-ep en-nam-be-lim i-na li-bi4-kà a-ší-tám um-ma a-ta-ma KÙ.BABBAR lá-qé am-ší-ma lá ak-nu-kà-šu KÙ.BABBAR i qá-tí-a lá-šu-ma al-qé 16 GÍN 10 5 ŠE KÙ.BABBAR i lu-qú-tim ša šé-ep DUMU ir-nu-id a-ší-tám ŠU.NÍGIN 2.83333 ma-na 7.66666 GÍN ṣa-ḫe-er-ma ší-tí KÙ.BABBAR-pì-kà 2.1666 GÍN gám-ra-kum
translation: From Aššur-idi to Aššur-nàdà: You sent me 2 minas less 1/2 shekel of silver with Istar-pilah. I took 1 mina 1/2 shekel out of what belongs to Sn-Istar. In all: 3 minas of silver. Thereof I paid 7 1/2 shekels as import duty; since the (money for the) transport tariff had been spent, I paid the balance of 1 shekel of silver. The rest of your silver amounts to 5/6 minas 1 1/2 shekel. I borrowed 8 shekels of silver from the son of Abu-salim and his partner. I still had a claim against you of 2/3 minas 1 1/4 shekels of silver, the proceeds from the sale of the textiles transported by Ennam-Bélum. You said: "The silver has been taken." But I forgot to seal it, (and) since I have no silver available to me I have taken it. I also still had a claim of 16 shekels 15 grains of silver from the goods transported by the son of Irnuid. In all: 2 5/6 minas 7 1/3 shekels have been deducted, so the rest of your silver is 1/6 shekels. It has been spent for you.

KÙ.BABBAR-pì-kà ša šé-ep be-lí-a-lim ù DUMU ku-um-ri-im a-pu-ul 3 LÁ 0.25 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR iš-tù ša-du-ú-tum gám-ra-at-ni a-na IŠTAR-pì-lá-aḫ ù DUMU ir-nu-it a-pu-ul 1 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR a-ší-im 2 GU4.ḪI.A 0.33333 GÍN i-na pá-né-e-ma šé-bi4-lam li-ba-kà lá tù-lá-ma-an a-na a-wi-li aḫ-ša-aḫ-ma al-qé
translation: From Aššur-idi to Aššur-nàdà: You left me 3 2/3 minas of silver; mina 1/2 shekel of silver belongs to "ii-Istar; 2 minas less 1/2 shekel of silver you sent to me with Istar-pilah; Dan-Aššur brought me 1 1/3 mina of refined silver—in all: 8 minas. Thereof I sent off to you goods worth 2 minas 16 2/3 shekels less 7 1/2 grains with Aššur-nàdà son of Irnuid; I further gave him 2 1/4 shekels, the wages of a donkey-driver. 1/2 shekels of silver to "n-Suen (and) 1/2 shekels to AsqûdiaI gave to them, they are bound by contract. If they have no claims on you, they must repay you in tin at the rate 16:1. Also Aššur-ré'i has been bound by contract (at the rate) 16:1 in tin for your remaining claim on him. Seize him to have him pay. After the price of the copper and shekel 15 grains of silver, which he owed to you, had been paid, I paid as balance to Aššur-ré'i 23 shekels rev. 22 1/2 grains of silver. 1/3 mina of silver, the price of a donkey, I paid to Usur-sa-Aššur. The day you left you took 5 minas of scrap metal worth 4 shekels of silver. The export duty was 1/2 shekel 15 grains; you wrote me about it, and I added it. As to the 5 1/2 minas of silver carried by Ennam-Alsur, 3 shekels were found to be missing during the check, but I forgot to charge it to you at the accounting. sheep belonging to Pidaya's son: 5 1/2 mina; 1 sheep worth 1 5/6 mina belonging to Iddin-ilum; sheep. 3 minas, sheep 2 1/3 minas 5 shekels, 1 sheep: 2 minas 10 shekels— belonging to Aslur-ré'i; 1 sheep worth 2 1/3 minas belonging to Zua; sheep worth 2 2/3 minas belonging to Dunnia; sheep worth 3 1/3 minas—the same Dunnia's eldest son. You forgot 1 textile worth 8 2/3 minas belonging to Aššur-lab, so I paid. The total in copper: 32 minas less shekels, in silver: 1/3 mina less 5/6 shekels, I have paid as the price of the textile and the sheep which you forgot about. I paid as compensation 1 [+ x] shekels after the transport tariff on your silver transported by Béli-âlum and the son of the priest. After the transport tariff had been spent I paid as compensation less 1/4 shekels of silver to Istar-pilah and Irnuid's son. 1 mina of silver I spent on the price of 2 oxen and grain to feed the sons. The rest of your silver: minas 2/3 shekels, I deducted from the 5 minas 1 shekel of silver, . the shipment transported by Ennam-Bélum; send the rest of my silver, 2 minas 1/3 shekels as soon as possible. Do not be angry. I wanted it for the men, so I took it.

transliteration: um-ma a-šur-i-dí-ma a-na a-šur-na-da qí-bi4-ma 5 GÍN KÙ.BABBAR i-na e-ra-bi4-šu-ma ás-qú-dí-a ša-áš-qí-il5-ma iš-tí ší-im ANŠE ku-nu-uk-ma i pá-ni-e-ma šé-bi4-lam 1 {TÚG}ku-ta-nu-um i ṭù-pì-a lá lá-pì-it ša a-šur-ta-ak-lá-ku ás-qú-dí-a-ma na-áš-a-kum
translation: From Aššur-idi to Aššur-nâdâ: Make Asgndia pay shekels of silver as soon as he arrives, seal that together with the money from the sale of the donkey,

transliteration: um-ma a-šùr-i-dí-ma a-na a-šùr-na-da qí-bi-ma a-šu-mì KÙ.BABBAR ša kà-ri-a ù ku-a-tí ša tù-šé-bi-lá-ni um-ma ša ki-ma kà-ri-a-ma lu-qú-tám a-na i-ta-aṭ-li-im-ma li-dí-in-ma KÙ.BABBAR lu-šé-bi-lam la ú-wa-šar a-pu-tum a-na i-ta-aṭ-li-im lu-qú-tám dì-in-ma mì-iš-li-šu KÙ.BABBAR šé-bi-lam ú-za-kà la i-ba-ší KÙ.BABBAR-ap a-wi-lim a-na na-ru-qí-im mì-ma la ta-na-ší ù šu-ma i-qí-šu-ni-kum mì-ma la ta-lá-qé šu-ma kà-ri-a i-na KÙ.BABBAR a-dí-im me-ra-šu i-na ša-ḫa-tí-kà li-zi-iz-ma ṭup-pá-am ša ší-a-ma-tim ší-ta-me-a-ma ší-ma-am dí-na-ma KÙ.BABBAR mì-iš-li-šu ku-un-kà-ma šé-bi-lá-nim ki-ma ú-ra-am a-na ma-mì-tim la i-ṣa-bu-tù-kà-ni KÙ.BABBAR i-na šé-bu-lim ší-bi šu-ku-un šu-ma um-ma me-ra-šu-ma lu-qú-tám dí-nam a-na a-wa-at É a-bi-a ma-aṣ-a-ku ma-a i-na a-wa-tim ip-tù-a-kà um-ma a-ta-ma ma-lá i-na É a-bi-kà i-ṣé-ri-a i-lá-qé-ú-ni i-na ṣé-ri-kà a-lá-qé ṭup-pá-kà ù ší-bi-kà da-ni-in lu-qú-tám wa-šé-er
translation: From Aššur-idi to Aššur-nâdâ: With regard to the silver belonging to Karria and yourself which you sent to me, Karria's representatives said: "Let him sell the goods for cash on delivery, and send the silver here! He must not release it (on credit)." Please, sell the goods for cash on delivery and send me his half of the silver raised. Do not listen to anything else! Do not use the man's silver as a joint-stock investment. Even if they are ready to give it as a gift you must not take any! If Karria has invested in the silver, then let his son assist you, and listen carefully to the letter concerning the purchases, sell the consignment and place his half of the silver under seal, and send it here. In order that they cannot seize you. tomorrow to make you swear an oath you must have witnesses when the silver is sent. If his son says: "Give me the goods! I shall be responsible for the affairs of my father's house!" —why, then he deprived you with words. You should answer: "Whatever they will take at my expense in your father's house, I shall take at your expense!" See to it that your tablet and your witnesses are in order! Release the goods.

transliteration: um-ma a-šur-i-dí-ma a-na a-šur-na-da qí-bi4-ma a-šu-mì i-dí-sú-en6 ša ta-áš-pu-ra-ni i-dí-sú-en6 aṣ-ba-at-ma i ma-ḫa-ar a-ḫi-ni um-ma a-na-ku-ma KÙ.BABBAR i-na ba-a-ba-tí-a ta-al-ta-qé KÙ.BABBAR-pì dí-nam um-ma šu-ut-ma mì-ma KÙ.BABBAR-áp-kà lá al-qé ú ni-kà-sí ú-ni-a-ni-ma KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ù 2 ma-na i-ṣé-ri-a il5-qé AN.NA 1 GÚ KI ša-lim-a-ḫi-im a-na u4-me ni-il5-qé-ma a-na pu-ru-uš-ḫa-tim ú-šé-ri-ib-ma iḫ-ta-al-qá-ni a-wa-at a-lim{ki} lá tí-de8 a-na-kam a-ba-ú-šu im-tí-du-ma a-ḫi-ni nu-šé-ší-ib-ma a-šu-mì KÙ.BABBAR a-na-kam ra-kà-sí ni-iš a-lim{ki} ú-ta-mì-ú-ni-a-tí-ma a-na 6 ma-na KÙ.BABBAR ú-ma-gi5-ru-ni-a-tí iš-tù ITU.1.KAM kán-bar-ta li-mu-um ib-ni-{d}IŠKUR DUMU ba-qú-ni-im 1 ma-na.TA i-na ša-tim KÙ.BABBAR ša-al-ma-am i-na a-lim{ki} i-ša-qá-lá-kum ša KÙ.BABBAR 1 ma-na ù 2 ma-na ší-ma-am na-ší a-ma-kam ṣa-ba-sú-ma ki-ma a-wi-lu-tí-kà e-pu-uš a-pu-tum ra-ma-kà za-ki-a-ma <gap> i-na ḫa-ar-pè iš-tí a-šur-ma-lik tí-ib-a-ma a-tal-kam šu-ma AN.NA a-na e-bu-ṭim ta-al-tí-qí-i a-na u4-me i-ṣé-er DAM.GÀR i-dí-šu a-pu-tum ì-lí-a-lúm iš-tí iš-té-en6 a-li-ki-im ṭur4-dam
translation: From Aššur-idi to Aššur-nàdà: Concerning Iddin-Suen about whom you wrote, I seized Iddin-Suen and in the presence of our colleagues I said: "You have taken silver from my outstanding claims! Give me my silver!" He answered: "I have not taken any of your silver." So, he cheated me with the account, for he has taken at least 1 or 2 minas of silver at my expense. We have borrowed tin, 1 talent, from alim-ahum on fixed terms, and he brought that into Burushaddum where it was lost for me. Don't you know the state of affairs in the City? Here his fathers have become numerous, so we convened our colleagues, and they made us swear an oath by the City concerning the contractual agreement to pay the silver here (in Assur), where they made us agree on 6 minas of silver: "Reckoned from the month Terfevxe"in the eponymy of Ibni-Adad son of Baqqunum he is to pay 1 mina per year without deduction to you in the City." He cariies goods worth at least 1 or 2 minas of silver. Seize him there and act as a gentleman. Please, free yourself of claims, set out and come at harvest time together with Aššur-malik If you have taken tin as an investment loan, then give it in commission to agents on fixed terms. Please, send me Ili-alum with the first travelers.

translation: From Aššur-idi to Aššur-nàdà: As if by the foot of divine Adad in full rush my house is devastated! But as for youyou have gone away! Please, please heed the words of the gods! Do not renounce the decision that the god has drawn up for you. If you renounce it, you will perish! The tin for which I was booked in the tablet, let them claim that at the accounting. Then you too may claim 6 minas 8 shekels of tin belonging to us at the accounting. Zuzu owes me 2 minas 13 1/3 shekels of silver. mina of silver is owed by amas-bàni son of Puzur-Ištar. 1/2 minas 5 shekels of silver is owed by Illil-bàni. The 1 mina of silver which he borrowed from you, the 1/2 mina which Aššur-samsi has brought to you—in tin it was minas—plus the 6 minas of tin that you will claim for the enterprise— take that, and then add in all minas 8 shekels of tin to the silver, seal it for the transportation, and send it to me as a greeting as soon as you arrive. If you are truly my brother, do not make me angry. If Samas-bàni is absent, then make available a corresponding amount of 1 mina of silver and send it to me. Please, let my share be deposited at the time of the accounting.

translation: 50 zamrutu, 2 5/6 minas of silver, 6 shekels of silver for sheep, all this I gave in Qattara. 1 sila of fine (oil) of ... for Ala-Adda ...; [I gave] 17 zamrutu for the caravan in Našila. (too broken for translation) ... 6 zamrutu ... in Hazura; all this I gave to the caravan.

translation: x sila of aromatic oil, 5 dulbatum and sundries I gave in Šerwun. shekels of tin I gave to the servant of the kaššum in Inum. bronze nails and 2 g., two-thirds mina of tin to the nākirum, I gave in Bābum. I paid 5 shekels of tin when I went on a mission. shekels of silver I paid in Burallum for wine after our accounts had been settled.

translation: [...] for wine. 3 shekels of tin, 5 dulbatum and sundries for Panaka. 5 dulbatum and sundries for Enlil-bāni in Humahum. One-third of a mina of copper for where our donkeys were standing in Humahum. 15 shekels of copper in Zurzu. Half a mina of copper to the massu’um of Ašihum. Half a mina minus half a shekel of tin, 10 dulbatum and 2 containers(?) of first quality oil and sundries for the palace, 5 shekels of tin, 5 dulbatum and one container(?) of first quality oil and sundries for the kaššum in Hurumhašum.

translation: 11 shekels of tin to the servant of Sam’al; 5 shekels of tin and a seal of haematite for his compagnon; 1 mina 2 shekels of tin in Enumgalim for ummanum. 5 beads of carnelian and 2 of zikanšarri for wood in Šunukam. 5 dulbatum, 1 muluhum and sundries, a quarter sila of aromatic oil, 6 ½ shekels of tin for the kaššum of Šunukam. 2 ¼ shekels of tin for wine. 1 ½ shekels of tin for the wife of the kaššum. 6 ½ shekels of tin for the massu’um in the mountain. 7 dulbatum, 1 muluhum and sundries, 5 shekels of tin to the wife of the brother of the king of Šehwa. 5/6 mina of tin to the palace of Šehwa; 2 shekels of tin to the great man; 3 shekels of tin to the smith; 3 shekels of tin for wine; 15 shekels of tin for the kaššum of Šehwa; half a mina and 5 shekels of tin for our guide – all this I gave upon <our> entering (the town). dulbatum, 2 nigirašum and sundries, 1/3 mina of copper to the elders, 15 shekels of copper to ...; all this in Tadhul. 3 shekels of tin for wine in Šuhru. 1 sila of aromatic oil, 10 dulbatum, an amahum, 1 muluhum, sundries and children’s <object> to the palace. 4 G. for the escort, 4 dulbatum and sundries, 2 shekels of tin for the priest. 3 1/3 shekels of tin for wine, 1 2/3 shekels of tin, also for wine; all this I gave in Burhum.

translation: 1 shekel of tin to Ilulāya. Half a shekel of tin is with Iliya. I gave him also half a mina of copper and sundries in Hirašta and one-third mina (of copper?) in Uršu. 10 shekels of tin and 10 dulbatum and sundries to the kaššum of Uršu. 1 ½ shekels of silver export-tax. 1 shekel of silver to the massu’um. 1 shekel of silver for a flask for the god(?). 2 shekels of tin to Sama. One-third mina of copper to Inib-Aššur. One-third mina of copper to Gallābum. 1 mina 10 shekels of copper in Bulbulhum. 5 dulbatum, 1 šinuntum and sundries to the overseer. One-third mina and 3+x shekels [...] to the massu’um. 1 abarnium-textile, a šitrum, 10 dulbatum to the king. Furthermore, [...] and 1 mina of tin to [...]. A šitrum, 10 dulbatum [...] and sundries to Šuku... . 1 mina 10 shekels of tin, a šitrum, 10 dulbatum, a muluhum and sundries to the kaššum [...]. 1 šitrum to the kaššum [...]. 3 ½ shekels of tin [...] ... to Madāya. All this I paid in Mama.

translation: 12 shekels of copper for the inn. 12 shekels of copper at the bridge. 12 shekels of copper in Bulbulhum. Half a mina of copper for garments. One-third shekel (of silver) for the inn in Aruwar. We took 10 shekels of silver for our food. Half a shekel of silver šaddu’atum-tax. Half a shekel of silver for the inn in Uršu. 2 shekels of silver were lost during refining.
